title: Time's Lesson.
content:
Mine enemy is growing old, --
    I have at last revenge.
    The palate of the hate departs;
    If any would avenge, --

    Let him be quick, the viand flits,
    It is a faded meat.
    Anger as soon as fed is dead;
    'T is starving makes it fat.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Emily Elizabeth Dickinson, titled "Time's Lesson.", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
